items.title: How a young doctor ended up being charged with manslaughter after street scuffle

children.content: A young Perth doctor and musician is behind bars, accused of killing a 63-year-old man after a Fremantle street scuffle. What do we know?

articles.title: Junior doctor faces court over death of man in Fremantle

children.content: A junior doctor from Perth's western suburbs appears in court charged with manslaughter following the death of a 63-year-old man near Fremantle's entertainment strip in the early hours yesterday.

items.title: Blind bowler's dream threatened by NDIS funding cuts

children.content: A blind bowler from regional WA say his dreams of representing Australia are being swept away along with his NDIS funding.

image.altText: A middle-aged man in a cap and sports tracksuit top stands in front of a bowling green.

items.title: Family of Perth man raises questions over his Bali death

children.content: The family of Perth man Cameron Hughes, who died in a Bali immigration detention centre, says it has many questions about the circumstances surrounding his death.

image.altText: A man in a pale blue shirt sitting at a venue holding a menu

items.title: 'Baby-making without baggage': Inside the world of social media semen donation

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

items.title: Golfers tee off for first aid to help save lives in WA's Wheatbelt

children.content: The long wait for an ambulance in WA's Wheatbelt makes first aid critical, and these golfers are ensuring their community is well prepared.

image.altText: Four people with golf clubs stand next to a hole

items.title: Agonising finish sees Lee denied another Scottish Open title

children.content: Min Woo Lee comes close to emulating his feats from 2021 but is denied by a bogey-free run-in from eventual winner Tom Kim.

image.altText: Min Woo Lee holds a golf ball in his right hand

items.title: Premier Roger Cook takes over defence industries in cabinet reshuffle

children.content: WA Premier Roger Cook will take on the defence industries and tourism portfolios in a cabinet reshuffle, following the departure of longstanding minister Paul Papalia.

gallery.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook stands behind a podium, with flags hanging behind him.

thumbnail.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook stands behind a podium, with flags hanging behind him.

items.title: 'Affordable' homes sit vacant more than seven months after completion

children.content: Governments across Australia are spending billions to boost the number of rentals available to people on low and middle incomes. A Four Corners investigation found many of these homes were unaffordable for those who needed them most, particularly single-income households.

image.altText: A man with a beard sits in an apartment. His facial expression is serious.

items.title: We're back to two competing superpowers and China is pulling ahead

children.content: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

image.altText: The US flag is waving in front of the red and gold round symbol of China

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Albanese has the Coalition exactly where he wants it

children.content: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: 'He continues to inspire us': Richard Scolyer's family pay emotional tribute

children.content: Richard Scolyer, a pioneer of cancer research, has been remembered for his "truly remarkable life" in a state memorial service at the Sydney Opera House.

image.altText: Picture of man on screen above stage with his belongs, inclduing a bike and Australian flags

items.title: How India uranium deal could unlock billions for resources powerhouse of WA

children.content: Australia has struck a uranium exports deal with India, which has put WA’s effective ban on uranium mining and exporting in the spotlight.

items.title: 'Psychopath' Tuchel gives England a chance to prove its mettle

children.content: This England World Cup squad is proving they are different to their predecessors at every stop. Now their manager has just given them their latest challenge.

image.altText: Thomas Tuchel speaks to Jude Bellingham

items.title: Book inspires Wajarri man to ditch mining for leadership

children.content: Board meetings and navigating native title were not on Kurt McIntosh's radar until he stumbled upon a book at the airport.

image.altText: Kurt is sitting on a tree root within a dry river bed holding Obama's book.  Kurt is wearing a grey jumper and purple shorts

items.title: Bazball: An uncomplicated idea leaving a very complex legacy

children.content: Brendon McCullum deliberately set out to make England's men's Test team a less complicated team to play cricket for. After four years of Bazball's highs and lows, it leaves a very complicated legacy.

items.title: History quiz: Test your recall on Tay Tay and the Tour de France

children.content: How much do you know about long-past British monarchs, the Salem witch trials and Nintendo games of the 80s? Find out with this week's quiz.

items.title: Numbat population improves amid warning there is a long way to go

children.content: Numbats, Western Australia's animal emblem, have been pulled from the brink of extinction after research found the population was growing in the wild.

items.title: Change to international travel as digital arrival cards roll out

children.content: A digital alternative to incoming passenger cards filled out by everyone entering Australia is to be implemented in the next two years.

image.altText: Two male passengers wheeling suitcases in an airport terminal.

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

image.altText: A group of young people drinking and laughing

items.title: Sinner triumphs over Zverev for second consecutive Wimbledon win

children.content: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

articles.title: Hewitt drops Wimbledon boys' title after epic battle against US star

children.content: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

image.altText: Cruz Hewitt looking frustrated while holding his racquet

articles.title: Nosková downs 'ex-friend' Muchová to win all-Czech Wimbledon final

children.content: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

image.altText: A young woman beams as she holds a giant sterling silver plate in front of a crowd.
